name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: How to build your first MCP server in 10 minutes
articles.summary: I built my first MCP server last week and it was way simpler than I expected. Here is exactly how, no fluff. Prerequisites Node.js 20+ 10 minutes Step 1: Scaffold npx create-mcp-server my-first-server cd my-first-server npm install This generates a complete TypeScript project with one example tool....

articles.title: Building an Agent-Native Data Layer over MCP
articles.summary: Agents are good at reasoning and bad at remembering how to talk to every data vendor. The usual pattern is a growing pile of one-off integrations: one client for markets, another for macro series, another for sanctions lists, another for weather. Each has its own auth, schema, rate limit, and...
articles.author: Arman Obosyan

articles.title: MCP goes stateless on July 28 — catch every breaking change in your server before the spec locks
articles.summary: On July 28, 2026 the Model Context Protocol locks its 2026-07-28 specification — the biggest breaking change the protocol has shipped. The handshake and sessions are gone; the protocol goes stateless. If you run an MCP server, several things you rely on today stop working on that date. I built...

articles.title: Claude Code MCP Setup: A Practical 2026 Guide
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is how Claude Code talks to external systems. GitHub, Slack, Linear, your database, your monitoring stack, and your browser can all become tool surfaces that Claude Code can call. articles.title: The agent should not choose its own state view
articles.summary: In part 5, I wrote about binding an intent to the state it was based on. If the branch moved after the agent read it, the request should stop. That check works. But it runs late. By the time a request arrives at the write path, the agent has already decided what to propose. That decision was made...

articles.title: MCP Token Cost Audit: PostgreSQL vs Google Maps vs GitHub vs Freee — 270 Tools Priced
articles.summary: PostgreSQL's MCP server costs 35 tokens to sit in your context. Freee's costs 17,500. Same protocol, same JSON, 500x apart before the agent has said a single word. I ran the same session boot against four production MCP servers — PostgreSQL, Google Maps, GitHub, and Freee — and priced the tool...

articles.title: My GitHub Token Is Valid. My MCP Server Still Gets a 403, and GitHub Never Saw the Request.
articles.summary: I maintain a small "Developer Presence" MCP server that wraps the GitHub and DEV.to REST APIs behind a handful of @mcp.tool() functions — get_github_profile, list_repos, get_repo_stats, plus the DEV.to publishing tools this exact blog runs on. articles.title: How to test agent experience changes without shipping them
articles.summary: Most changes you think will improve AI agent behavior won't. We tested a dozen hypotheses on a real project upgrade scenario and the majority failed. articles.title: Join us for our MCP Live! — A free livestream covering all things MCP
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is the open standard for connecting AI models to tools and data. It was first introduced to the world by Anthropic in November 2024, and is now the most widely adopted standard in the world of Generative AI. You can now use MCP servers to connect agents to your data...
articles.author: Pamela_Fox

articles.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
articles.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. articles.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
articles.summary: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
articles.author: Brooke Mosby

articles.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
articles.summary: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
articles.author: Will Papper

articles.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
articles.summary: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
